fact: Genesys Aerosystems and XP Services announced FAA Supplemental Type Certification (STC) approval of the Genesys modernized avionics suite for the UH-60A and EH-60A Black Hawk on October 21, 2020. The STC remains active and the program has since expanded: as of February 2024, Genesys Aerosystems (a Moog company) is working with XP Services to add the UH-60L model to the existing Black Hawk Alpha model STC.

The suite upgrade replaces legacy gyros, which are prone to failure and costly to repair, with solid-state, light-weight, digital Attitude/Heading Reference System. (Genesys Aerosystems / XP Services joint press release, October 21, 2020, still live at genesys-aerosystems.com; reaffirmed verbatim in Moog/Genesys UH-60L expansion announcement, February 27, 2024, at moog.com and genesys-aerosystems.com.)

Rodney Allison, President & CEO — a graduate of the U.S. Naval Test Pilot School with 20+ years of U.S. Army Special Operations aviation experience, Mr. Allison maintains over 20 FAA type certificates in both rotary and fixed wing aircraft, has 14,000+ flight hours, and is qualified in over 140 different aircraft. He also holds current Airline Transport Pilot, Flight Engineer, and Certified Flight Instructor Certification and is a FAA Designated Engineering Representative as well as an associate fellow of The Society of Experimental Test Pilots. Source: https://xpservices.us/about/ (live, indexed November 2024; consistent with LinkedIn and RocketReach records confirming U.S. Naval Test Pilot School attendance and U.S. Army Experimental Test Pilot background).

He also holds current Airline Transport Pilot, Flight Engineer, and Certified Flight Instructor Certification and is a FAA Designated Engineering Representative as well as an associate fellow of The Society of Experimental Test Pilots. (Source: https://xpservices.us/about/, cached November 18, 2024)

When we lost Rucie Moore all too soon in 2019, his legacy of love for aviation was preserved within his design for our original company logo. It symbolizes his passion for flight and to never stop growing both professionally and personally. XP Services has since expanded on his inspiration through a logo evolution that all began with Rucie. He served as an experimental test pilot and business development executive for XP Services, and was a U.S. Army Master Aviator (Chief Warrant Officer 5) who died on April 16, 2019 in a private helicopter crash near Fountain Hills, Arizona.

The XPS Tullahoma Airport (KTHA) facility provides Avionics, FAA Part 145 Repair Station, and a DCMA compliant hangar facility with machining capability to fabricate components for rapid-prototyping. (Source: Genesys Aerosystems press release, archived August 22, 2024; corroborated by multiple Genesys releases, City of Tullahoma municipal airport page, xpservices.us live site, and LinkedIn profile.)

Recoil Aerospace Inc. (Recoil), designer of the world's most advanced aerial fire suppression systems (AFSS), has announced Tennessee-based XP Services (XP) as a new distributor for the Recoil line of AFSS tank systems for the UH-60 Black Hawk series helicopters. Announced May 8, 2025. Source: recoil-usa.com (first-party Recoil press release, also confirmed by HeliHub, RotorHub, Vertical Mag, AirMed&Rescue, and Vertical Aviation International, all May 2025).

Nearly all of our professionals are veterans of the armed forces with well over 100 years of military and special operations experience. (Source: https://xpservices.us/about/, cached November 18, 2024, confirmed live via web search June 26, 2026)
